text: Or perhaps ‘holy person’. The Greek text has, ‘hyena’.

Literally, ‘in the mouth of a sinner’. This would naturally be interpreted as meaning ‘in the estimation of a sinner’, but such a rendering would give no parallel of thought between the two halves of the verse.

Or possibly the sense is that it is difficult to find instances of the pleasant mien that results from a generous heart, because they are so rare. If so, the first two verses of ch. 14 should be taken as part of this chapter.
